game_name: Spider-Man: Return of the Sinister Six

danger_signals.visual_form: Level endpoint with enlarged villain sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Player approaching mandatory combat with Sinister Six member; escalation of threat intensity

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual projectile sprites traveling across screen space
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger requiring evasion; incoming damage threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Power bar reduction visible on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increasing; critical state approaching

danger_signals.visual_form: Vertical wall sections, environmental hazards in platforming paths
danger_signals.meaning: Mechanical danger requiring precise movement to avoid failure

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite disappearance or collapse animation
safety_signals.meaning: Threat neutralized; temporary safety window created

safety_signals.visual_form: Power bar refill upon enemy defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Health recovery opportunity; viable survival path demonstrated
Advancement toward objective; player agency and progress
Resource availability; capability enhancement for combat readiness

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Spider emblem on suit; web-based visual effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spider-Man sprite color scheme distinguishes from enemies; web projectiles visually unique

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual villain visual identity per member (Electro, Sandman, Mysterio, Vulture, Hobgoblin, Doctor Octopus)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each boss has distinct sprite design, attack patterns, and behavioral animations reflecting comic-source characters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss encounters at level endpoints; villain-specific level theming (e.g., electrical effects for Electro, sand effects for Sandman)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mechanical tentacle appendages; octopus visual motif
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Final boss with enhanced visual complexity; regenerating health bar (NES version shows multiple health segments vs. Master System single bar)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level 6 endpoint; positioned as supreme threat in narrative hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single life resource; basic enemy density; straightforward level design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies follow predictable patrol patterns; limited projectile complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy variety; environmental hazards introduce vertical complexity; bosses require pattern recognition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy attack frequency increases; boss regeneration mechanics introduce resource management pressure

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final confrontation - Doctor Octopus as world threat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite dominates screen space; distinctive mechanical octopus design signals apex danger
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple health phases (NES version); complex attack patterns requiring learned response sequences

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Master System port - reduced difficulty state
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same visual assets but repositioned item placement; simplified enemy encounters; single health bar bosses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fewer enemy instances per level; easier platforming geometry; reduced regeneration mechanics

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Game Gear version - cramped visibility state
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller viewport window showing reduced screen portion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced visual warning time for incoming projectiles; increased difficulty through information asymmetry rather than mechanical changes

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of six levels terminates with specific Sinister Six member boss fight, creating implicit narrative progression from minor to major threats
Villain-specific environmental aesthetics (electrical hazards for Electro, sandstorm effects for Sandman, etc.) communicate threat identity without explicit exposition
Intermediate objectives (key/detonator retrieval) signal plot progression and world-state changes through mechanical narrative requirements
Each villain rendered with comic-accurate visual design, immediately communicating Marvel franchise authenticity and character role
Single life with one continue creates implicit 'world is hostile' state; no health restoration items communicate unforgiving threat environment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full movement capability (jump, punch, kick, duck, climb, web-shoot); responsive control
Power bar visually depletes; gameplay remains unchanged but visual feedback indicates vulnerability
Patrol movement patterns; predictable sprite animation cycles
Projectile attack emission; sprite animation acceleration; movement toward/away from player based on AI routine
Multiple attack patterns; electrical projectile emission; complex movement behavior indicating high threat
Regenerating health bar (multiple segments); coordinated tentacle attack patterns; screen-dominant sprite positioning
Single health bar; simplified attack pattern; reduced regeneration removes mechanical complexity

notes: Limited source documentation (T3 tier) constrains manifestation pattern extraction. Sources focus on development history (unused graphics) and port comparison mechanics rather than visual communication analysis. No explicit data on color schemes, HUD design, or subtle behavioral tells. Environmental storytelling inferred from gameplay structure rather than documented design intent. NES/Master System/Game Gear version differences reveal design-space decisions regarding difficulty communication through mechanic simplification rather than visual language changes. Spider-Man's unused ceiling-crawl mechanic suggests original design concept (potentially affecting environmental threat communication) was cut.
